verification.note: The Rs 10,000 threshold, the 90% payment threshold, and the 1% per month rate are long-standing, unchanged provisions of Sections 208 and 234B. Section 234C's instalment calendar (15%/45%/75%/100% by 15 June/15 Sep/15 Dec/15 Mar), the 12%/36% safe harbours for the first two instalments, the 3/3/3/1 month interest periods, and the exclusion of dividend and capital-gains income that arose after an instalment date are likewise long-standing provisions of Section 234C. Checked against multiple current filing guides; re-check only if a Budget/Finance Act amendment is reported.

source_refs: Income Tax Act, 1961, Section 208 (liability to pay advance tax)
Income Tax Act, 1961, Section 234B (interest for default in payment of advance tax)
Income Tax Act, 1961, Section 234C (interest for deferment of advance tax instalments)

section_234c.later_income_caveat: Dividends and capital gains only count toward the instalments due after they actually arrived, and no Section 234C interest applies to them if the next instalment covered the resulting tax. This estimate spreads your whole year's tax across all four instalments, so if part of your tax comes from gains or dividends that arrived mid-year, the true figure is lower. Treat this as the ceiling, not the bill.
